title: Creating Repositories
content:
### New Repo via Web UI

Click the **+** icon in the top-right corner → **New repository**. Enter a name, optional description, choose visibility, and optionally initialize with a README.

### Pushing an Existing Repository

```bash
# Add remote origin
git remote add origin https://github.com/username/repo.git

# Push existing code
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main
```

### Repo Visibility

- **Public** — Anyone on the internet can see it
- **Private** — Only you and invited collaborators can see it

### Repository Templates

You can create a template repository (Settings → **Template repository**) so others can generate new repos with the same structure, files, and branch settings.
codeExample:
git remote add origin https://github.com/username/repo.git
git branch -M main
git push -u origin main

quiz.question: What command adds a GitHub remote to an existing local repo?
How do you make a repo usable as a template for future projects?
